text: Machine learning is a way for computers to learn from data. In supervised learning, the computer is given training data, which includes examples with correct answers. For instance, you might show it pictures of cats and dogs, each labeled correctly. The computer uses this data to build a model, which is like a set of rules it creates. Once the model is ready, it can make predictions on new data it has never seen before. To check how well the model works, we measure its accuracy. High accuracy means the model is good at predicting the right answers, while low accuracy suggests it needs more training or improvement.
